title: oxalis corniculata subsp. albicans
intro: Oxalis corniculata subsp. albicans, also known as the white wood sorrel or white creeping wood sorrel, is a subspecies of the oxalis corniculata plant, a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of oxalis corniculata subsp. albicans include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Oxalis corniculata subsp. albicans is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
